# AuthCSES SDK

> Java SDK for SpiceDB permission management. Direct gRPC connection, no platform dependency.

## Docs

- [SDK Guide](src/main/resources/META-INF/authcses-sdk/GUIDE.md): Complete API reference, configuration, usage patterns
- [SDK CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Coding standards, architecture, constraints
- [Design Spec](docs/specs/2026-04-03-sdk-v2-architecture.md): V2 architecture design

## Instructions for AI Agents

### Do

- Use `AuthCsesClient.builder().target("...").presharedKey("...").build()` to initialize
- Use `AuthCsesClient.inMemory()` for unit tests
- Use fluent API: `client.resource(type, id).check(permission).by(userId)`
- Use `ConnectionConfig` for connection settings, separate from business logic
- Register per-resource-type strategies via `ResourceStrategy` interface
- Close the client with try-with-resources or `registerShutdownHook(true)`

### Do Not

- Do not pass platform URLs — SDK connects directly to SpiceDB
- Do not use `apiKey` or `namespace` — these are V1 concepts, removed
- Do not hardcode string literals for resource types/permissions — use codegen constants or `ResourceStrategy`
- Do not call `check()` with a relation name — relations are for `grant/revoke`, permissions are for `check/who/lookup`
- Do not create multiple `AuthCsesClient` instances — one singleton per application

### API Pattern

```
grant:    resource(type, id).grant(relation).to(userIds)
revoke:   resource(type, id).revoke(relation).from(userIds)
check:    resource(type, id).check(permission).by(userId) → CheckResult
checkAll: resource(type, id).checkAll(perms...).by(userId) → PermissionSet
who:      resource(type, id).who().withPermission(p).fetch() → List<String>
lookup:   lookup(type).withPermission(p).by(userId).fetch() → List<String>
batch:    resource(type, id).batch().grant(r).to(u).revoke(r).from(u).execute()
```

### Consistency

Default is SESSION (write-after-read safe). Override per-operation with `.withConsistency()` or per-type via `ResourceStrategy`.

### Error Handling

```
AuthCsesAuthException       → invalid credentials (don't retry)
AuthCsesConnectionException → network issue (auto-retried)
AuthCsesTimeoutException    → timeout (auto-retried)
CircuitBreakerOpenException → SpiceDB down (fast-fail)
InvalidResourceException    → typo in resource type (fix code)
InvalidPermissionException  → typo or relation/permission confusion (fix code)
```
